title: Minimum number of platforms required for a railway

content.problemStatement: We are given two arrays that represent the arrival and departure times of trains that stop at the platform. We need to find the minimum number of platforms needed at the railway station so that no train has to wait.
content.inputFormat: Two arrays of integers representing arrival and departure times of trains
content.outputFormat: The minimum number of platforms required

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the arrival and departure times, then use two pointers to find the maximum number of overlapping intervals
solution.timeComplexity: O(n log n) due to sorting
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) if sorting is done in-place

code.Python3:
# Class to group the solution method
class Solution:
    
    # Function to count minimum platforms needed
    def countPlatforms(self, n, arr, dep):

        # Initialize answer to 1
        ans = 1

        # Loop over all arrival times
        for i in range(n):

            # Initialize count of overlapping intervals
            count = 1

            # Check overlap with every other train
            for j in range(i + 1, n):

                # Check if there is overlap between train i and j
                if (arr[i] >= arr[j] and arr[i] <= dep[j]) or \
                   (arr[j] >= arr[i] and arr[j] <= dep[i]):
                    count += 1

            # Update maximum platform count
            ans = max(ans, count)

        return ans


# Main execution
arr = [900, 945, 955, 1100, 1500, 1800]
dep = [920, 1200, 1130, 1150, 1900, 2000]
n = len(arr)

# Create object and call the function
obj = Solution()
print("Minimum number of Platforms required", obj.countPlatforms(n, arr, dep))
